18	// queue drain. ddx work treats --harness,

21	// Retry-power policy between attempts is owned here, not in the agent service.
22	func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkCommand() *cobra.Command {
23		cmd := &cobra.Command{
24			Use:   "work",
25			Short: "Drain the bead execution queue",
26			Long: `work drains the execution-ready bead queue. It is the FEAT-010 layer-3
27	queue drain: it iterates ddx try (layer 2) across ready beads until a stop
28	condition is met and owns retry-power policy between attempts.
29	
30	ddx work treats --harness, --provider, --model, and --profile as opaque
31	passthrough constraints forwarded to Fizeau unchanged. DDx does not validate
32	these values or branch on them; Fizeau owns routing within the requested power
33	bounds.
34	
35	Review is on by default. --no-review is a break-glass override and
36	requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing. A bead label of review:skip
37	is only honored when it also carries a sibling review:skip-reason:*
38	label; otherwise the label is ignored.
39	
40	Stop conditions (evaluated between attempts):
41	  drained     — no ready beads remain
42	  blocked     — every remaining bead has produced a terminal non-success outcome
43	  deferred    — configured budget exhausted
44	  no_progress — N consecutive attempts produced no commit (default N=3)
45	  signal      — SIGINT/SIGTERM received between attempts
46	
47	work runs inline in the current process; per ADR-022 there is no separate
48	"submit to server" mode. The legacy --local flag is accepted but ignored
49	(deprecation warning printed) and will be removed in a future release.
50	`,
51			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue and exit
52	  ddx work
53	
54	  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
55	  ddx work --once
56	
57	  # Watch for newly-ready work after the current queue drains

59

63	  # Forward harness/model as passthrough constraints (ddx does not validate these)
64	  ddx work --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7
65	
66	  # Skip review only with the break-glass acknowledgement flag
67	  ddx work --once --no-review --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing
68	
69	  # Constrain power powerClass (retry-power policy is owned by ddx work)
70	  ddx work --once --min-power 40 --max-power 90`,
71			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
72			RunE: f.runWork,

129	// runWork is the RunE for "ddx work". It drives the FEAT-010 layer-3 queue
130	// drain with harness/provider/model treated as opaque passthrough constraints —
131	// ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService is not called; DDx does not inspect or
132	// branch on those values. Retry-power policy (min-power / max-power) is owned
133	// by this layer.
134	func (f *CommandFactory) runWork(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
135		if err := f.checkLifecycleMigrationGate(cmd); err != nil {
136			return err
137		}
138	
139		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
140		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
141		lockmetrics.SetSink(lockmetrics.FileSink(projectRoot))
142		lockmetrics.SetCapEnforcement(projectRoot, "")
143		f.warnIfInstalledBinaryBehindSource(cmd)
144	
145		// Preflight: warn once per process for degraded project-local skill layout.
146		// Suppressed in JSON mode to avoid corrupting machine-readable output
147		// (stderr and stdout are merged by some test helpers and log aggregators).
148		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
149		if !asJSON {
150			preflightResult := checkProjectRuntimePreflight(projectRoot)
151			f.preflightWarnOnce.Do(func() {
152				emitPreflightWarning(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), preflightResult)
153			})
154		}
155	
156		return f.runAgentExecuteLoopImpl(cmd, true, "")
157	}

239	func countWorkTerminalOutcomes(result *agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult) (closed, changed, alreadySatisfied, preserved int) {
240		for _, attempt := range result.Results {
241			switch attempt.Status {
242			case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess:
243				closed++
244				changed++
245			case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied:
246				closed++
247				alreadySatisfied++
248			case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview:
249				preserved++
250			}
251		}
252		if closed == 0 && result.Successes > 0 {
253			closed = result.Successes
254		}
255		return closed, changed, alreadySatisfied, preserved
256	}
257	
258	func preservedWorkAttempts(result *agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult) []agent.ExecuteBeadReport {
259		if result == nil {
260			return nil
261		}
262		var out []agent.ExecuteBeadReport
263		for _, attempt := range result.Results {
264			if attempt.Status == agent.ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview {
265				out = append(out, attempt)
266			}
267		}
268		return out
269	}
270	
271	func failedWorkAttempts(result *agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult) []agent.ExecuteBeadReport {
272		if result == nil {
273			return nil
274		}
275		var out []agent.ExecuteBeadReport
276		for _, attempt := range result.Results {
277			switch attempt.Status {
278			case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
279				agent.ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied,
280				agent.ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview:
281				continue
282			default:
283				out = append(out, attempt)
284			}
285		}
286		return out
287	}

289	func workExitSummary(result *agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult) string {
290		switch result.ExitReason {
291		case "drained":
292			return "drained current execution-ready queue"
293		case "once_complete":
294			return "once complete"
295		case "budget":
296			return "budget reached"
297		case "no_progress":
298			return "no-progress policy stopped work"
299		case "blocked":
300			return "blocked waiting for external action"
301		case "operator_attention":
302			return "operator attention required"
303		case "sigint", "sigterm", "context_cancelled":
304			return "stopped by signal"
305		case "fatal_config":
306			return "fatal configuration error"
307		case "preflight_failed":
308			return "preflight failed"
309		case "resource_exhausted":
310			return "resource exhausted"
311		case "routing_unavailable":
312			return "routing unavailable"
313		}
314		if result.StopCondition != "" {
315			return strings.ToLower(strings.ReplaceAll(result.StopCondition, "_", " "))
316		}
317		return "completed"
318	}

320	func writeQueueSnapshotTerminalSummary(w io.Writer, snapshot *agent.QueueSnapshot) {
321		if snapshot == nil {
322			return
323		}

329			snapshot.ExecutionReadyCount,

331			snapshot.ProposedOperatorAttentionCount,
332			snapshot.HumanReviewBlockerCount,
333			snapshot.RetryCooldownCount,
334			retry,
335			snapshot.ExecutionIneligibleCount,
336			snapshot.SupersededCount,
337			snapshot.SkippedEpicsCount,
338			snapshot.EpicClosureCandidatesCount,
339		)
340		if snapshot.HumanReviewBlockedTotal <= 0 || snapshot.HumanReviewBlockerCount <= 0 {
341			return
342		}
343		fmt.Fprintf(w, "%d beads blocked behind %d needs-human blockers:\n",
344			snapshot.HumanReviewBlockedTotal, snapshot.HumanReviewBlockerCount)
345		for i, blocker := range snapshot.HumanReviewBlockers {
346			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  %d. %s %s (%d downstream)\n", i+1, blocker.ID, blocker.Title, blocker.DownstreamBlockedCount)
347		}

350	func writeNoReadyWorkSummary(w io.Writer, projectRoot string, d agent.NoReadyWorkBreakdown) {
351		if projectRoot != "" {
352			fmt.Fprintf(w, "project: %s\n", projectRoot)
353		}
354		fmt.Fprintln(w, "No execution-ready beads.")

385		}
386		if len(d.Epics) > 0 {

390		if len(d.EpicClosureCandidates) > 0 {

405			len(d.EpicClosureCandidates) == 0 &&
406			d.NextRetryAfter == ""

162	// TestWork_NetworkFreeDrain_NoFetchInLoop verifies reliability principle P9:
163	// the drain loop's pre-claim hook never blocks on network I/O. The fixture
164	// repo has an origin remote whose URL points at a deleted path; the last
165	// observed refs/remotes/origin/main remains as a cached tip. A network-free
166	// drain must claim and execute a ready bead without error even though no
167	// `git fetch` against origin could succeed.
168	func TestWork_NetworkFreeDrain_NoFetchInLoop(t *testing.T) {
169		t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
170	
171		gitEnv := gitpkg.CleanEnv()
172		runGitEnv := func(dir string, args ...string) {
173			t.Helper()
174			c := exec.Command("git", args...)
175			c.Dir = dir
176			c.Env = gitEnv
177			out, err := c.CombinedOutput()
178			require.NoError(t, err, "git %v: %s", args, out)
179		}
180	
181		originDir := t.TempDir()
182		workDir := t.TempDir()
183		runGitEnv(originDir, "init", "--bare", "-b", "main")
184		runGitEnv(workDir, "clone", originDir, ".")
185		runGitEnv(workDir, "config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
186		runGitEnv(workDir, "config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
187		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "seed.txt"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644))
188		runGitEnv(workDir, "add", "seed.txt")
189		runGitEnv(workDir, "commit", "-m", "chore: initial seed")
190		runGitEnv(workDir, "push", "-u", "origin", "main")
191	
192		// Break origin: point the remote URL at a deleted path and remove the bare
193		// repo. refs/remotes/origin/main survives as the last-observed tip, so a
194		// network-free ancestry check still resolves, but any fetch would fail.
195		deletedOrigin := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "gone")
196		runGitEnv(workDir, "remote", "set-url", "origin", "file://"+deletedOrigin)
197		require.NoError(t, os.RemoveAll(originDir))
198	
199		seedExecuteBead(t, workDir, &bead.Bead{
200			ID:        "ddx-network-free-drain",
201			Title:     "network-free drain bead",
202			Status:    bead.StatusOpen,
203			Priority:  0,
204			IssueType: bead.DefaultType,
205		})
206	
207		var executed []string
208		factory := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
209		factory.AgentRunnerOverride = &tryHookRunnerStub{t: t}
210		factory.tryExecutorOverride = agent.ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (agent.ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
211			executed = append(executed, beadID)
212			return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
213				BeadID:    beadID,
214				Status:    agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
215				ResultRev: "rev-" + beadID,
216			}, nil
217		})
218	
219		out, err := executeCommand(
220			factory.NewRootCommand(),
221			"work",
222			"--once",
223			"--project", workDir,
224			"--no-review",
225			"--no-review-i-know-what-im-doing",
226		)
227		require.NoError(t, err, "network-free drain must claim and drain without origin reachability: %s", out)
228		assert.Equal(t, []string{"ddx-network-free-drain"}, executed, "the ready bead must be claimed and executed")
229	}

631	// ddx-e0b95b4a. agent.endpoints declares where providers live (transport
632	// config) — it does NOT pin a routing decision the way agent.model does.
633	// Treating endpoints as a routing pin disables zero-config powerClass inference
634	// (autoInferPowerClass in runAgentExecuteLoopImpl), so no-flag `ddx work` sends an
635	// empty Policy and Fizeau resolves it via its default policy. In production
636	// at /home/erik/Projects/ddx this scored Opus on ordinary implementation work
637	// (see .ddx/attachments/ddx-c3219628/events.jsonl: actual_model=opus,
638	// cost_usd=15.0776 on a worker-status bug; ddx-6cde5ffd: $6.83 on a registry
639	// package task).
640	//
641	// The matrix verifies the new contract: only an explicit model pin in
642	// agent.config.yaml suppresses zero-config powerClass inference; endpoints, an
643	// empty agent block, or no config at all leave inference active so the
644	// implementation profile selector can request a Fizeau policy by metadata.
645	func TestProjectHasRoutingConfig_EndpointsAreTransportNotRoutingPin(t *testing.T) {
646		cases := []struct {
647			name     string

651		}{
652			{
653				name:     "no .ddx/config.yaml",
654				writeCfg: false,
655				want:     false,
656			},
657			{
658				name:     "library-only config (zero-config baseline)",
659				writeCfg: true,
660				yaml: `version: "1.0"
661	library:
662	  path: ".ddx/plugins/ddx"
663	  repository:
664	    url: "https://example.com/lib"
665	    branch: "main"
666	`,
667				want: false,

672				yaml: `version: "1.0"
673	library:
674	  path: ".ddx/plugins/ddx"

676	    url: "https://example.com/lib"
677	    branch: "main"
678	agent:
679	  endpoints:
680	    - type: lmstudio
681	      host: 127.0.0.1
682	      port: 1234
683	    - type: omlx
684	      host: 127.0.0.1
685	      port: 1235
686	`,
687				want: false,

696	    url: "https://example.com/lib"
697	    branch: "main"

923		// this bead's evidence contract.
924		statusOut, statusErr := exec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "status", "--porcelain").CombinedOutput()
925		require.NoError(t, statusErr, string(statusOut))
926		var dirty []string
927		for _, line := range strings.Split(strings.TrimSpace(string(statusOut)), "\n") {
928			if strings.TrimSpace(line) == "" || strings.Contains(line, ".ddx/metrics/") {
929				continue
930			}
931			dirty = append(dirty, line)
932		}
933		require.Empty(t, dirty,
934			"worktree must be clean after a provider_connectivity attempt; output=%q\nstatus:\n%s", out, statusOut)
935	
936		// AC #1: any execution-evidence bundle that survived to the project root is
937		// committed (tracked), never left untracked. A clean worktree (above) plus
938		// a present-and-tracked or absent bundle satisfies the contract.
939		logOut, logErr := exec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "log", "--all", "--name-only", "--pretty=format:").CombinedOutput()
940		require.NoError(t, logErr, string(logOut))
941		execDir := filepath.Join(dir, ddxroot.DirName, "executions")
942		if entries, statErr := os.ReadDir(execDir); statErr == nil {
943			for _, e := range entries {
944				if !e.IsDir() {
945					continue
946				}
947				for _, name := range []string{"manifest.json", "prompt.md", "result.json"} {
948					if _, ferr := os.Stat(filepath.Join(execDir, e.Name(), name)); ferr != nil {
949						continue
950					}
951					tracked := filepath.ToSlash(filepath.Join(ddxroot.DirName, "executions", e.Name(), name))
952					require.Contains(t, string(logOut), tracked,
953						"evidence file %s present on disk must be committed", tracked)
954				}
955			}
956		}
957	
958		// AC #3: route-failure evidence is recorded on the bead with provider,
959		// model, reason, and error detail.
960		events, evErr := store.Events(beadID)
961		require.NoError(t, evErr)
962		var routeFailure *bead.BeadEvent
963		for i := range events {
964			if events[i].Kind == "route-failure" {
965				routeFailure = &events[i]
966			}
967		}
968		require.NotNil(t, routeFailure, "expected a kind=route-failure event recorded on the bead")
969		require.Contains(t, routeFailure.Body, "grendel-omlx", "route-failure event must record the failed provider")
970		require.Contains(t, routeFailure.Body, "provider_connectivity", "route-failure event must record the outcome reason")
971		require.Contains(t, routeFailure.Body, "i/o timeout", "route-failure event must record the transport error detail")
972	}

12	const gracefulCancelMessage = "Cancel received, shutting down gracefully"
13	
14	// newGracefulCancelAnnouncer returns a one-shot notifier for root-level
15	// cancellation. It prints the operator-visible shutdown line exactly once and
16	// then calls stop so a second SIGINT/SIGTERM falls back to the default signal
17	// disposition and can still kill a wedged cleanup path.
18	func newGracefulCancelAnnouncer(out io.Writer, stop func()) func() {
19		var once sync.Once
20		return func() {
21			once.Do(func() {
22				_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(out, gracefulCancelMessage)
23			})
24			if stop != nil {
25				stop()
26			}
27		}
28	}
29	
30	// executeRootCommand runs the root command under the supplied context while a
31	// background watcher translates cancellation into the graceful shutdown
32	// message. Tests can call this with a cancellable context directly instead of
33	// delivering real OS signals.
34	func executeRootCommand(rootCmd *cobra.Command, ctx context.Context, stop func()) error {
35		cancelAnnounce := newGracefulCancelAnnouncer(rootCmd.ErrOrStderr(), stop)
36	
37		done := make(chan struct{})
38		go func() {
39			select {
40			case <-ctx.Done():
41				cancelAnnounce()
42			case <-done:
43			}
44		}()
45		defer close(done)
46	
47		err := rootCmd.ExecuteContext(ctx)
48		if ctx.Err() != nil {
49			cancelAnnounce()
50		}
51		return err
52	}
